findings.evidence: `rg` finds Mathlib’s bridge at `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:161-163`: `charFun μ t = VectorFourier.fourierIntegral fourierChar μ (innerₗ E) 1 (-(2 * π)⁻¹ • t)`; the new proof at `TauCeti/Analysis/Bochner/FourierConvention.lean:54-59` is exactly `rw [charFun_eq_fourierIntegral']` followed by scalar simplification.
findings.fix: Use `MeasureTheory.charFun_eq_fourierIntegral'` at call sites, e.g. rewrite the `charFun μ (-(2 * Real.pi) • a)` side and close the frequency equality by `simp`/scalar simplification, instead of adding a new public theorem.
findings.issue: `fourierIntegral_one_eq_charFun_neg_two_pi_smul` is reprovable by specializing and reversing Mathlib’s existing `MeasureTheory.charFun_eq_fourierIntegral'` plus scalar simplification, so this is parallel API rather than new material.

summary: The PR adds only a named algebraic reorientation of Mathlib’s existing characteristic-function/Fourier-integral bridge, creating parallel API instead of reusing the existing lemma.
